hero_description: Master the fundamentals of stock market investing and build a strong foundation in financial literacy. This course teaches you how to understand stock exchanges, analyze companies, and make informed investment decisions in the Indian market. Perfect for beginners who want to grow wealth through smart investing.
overview_description: This comprehensive 8-week program covers everything from basic stock market concepts to practical investment strategies. You will learn how NSE and BSE work, how to read financial statements, analyze stocks using fundamental and technical analysis, manage investment risk, and build a diversified portfolio. Through real-world case studies and live market examples, you'll gain practical skills to start investing confidently in Indian stocks.

modules.title: Stock Market Fundamentals & Indian Financial System
topics: What is a stock market and why it matters — Understanding equity ownership, wealth creation, and economic growth in India
NSE (National Stock Exchange) vs BSE (Bombay Stock Exchange) — Key differences, trading hours, listing requirements, and market structure
Stock market participants — Investors, traders, brokers, regulators (SEBI), and their roles in Indian markets
Types of securities — Shares (equity), bonds, debentures, mutual funds, and ETFs available in India
How to open a Demat and Trading account — Step-by-step process with major brokers like Zerodha, Angel, HDFC Securities
SEBI regulations and investor protection — Rules for retail investors, complaint redressal, and legal safeguards
Stock market indices — Understanding SENSEX (BSE 30), NIFTY 50, NIFTY 100, and what they represent
Market cycles and economic indicators — Bull markets, bear markets, GDP, inflation, and interest rates affecting Indian stocks
Cost of investing — Brokerage charges, taxes (STT, capital gains), and hidden costs to avoid
Historical context of Indian stock market — Growth from 1875 to 2024, key events, and lessons for investors

modules.title: Understanding Stocks, Shares & Company Valuation
topics: What is a share and equity ownership — How buying shares makes you a company owner with voting rights
Face value, market price, and book value — Understanding different valuations of Indian company stocks
Dividends and bonus shares — How companies reward shareholders and impact on investment returns
Rights issues and stock splits — Corporate actions that affect your shareholding in Indian companies
Market capitalization (Market Cap) — Large-cap, mid-cap, small-cap stocks and their characteristics
Earnings Per Share (EPS) — Calculating and interpreting EPS for Indian companies like TCS, Infosys, Reliance
Price-to-Earnings (P/E) Ratio — How to use P/E ratio to identify overvalued and undervalued stocks
Price-to-Book (P/B) Ratio — Understanding asset value and when to buy stocks trading below book value
Dividend Yield — Calculating returns from dividends for income-focused investing in Indian stocks
Return on Equity (ROE) — Measuring how efficiently companies use shareholder money
Understanding Balance Sheet, Income Statement, Cash Flow Statement — Reading and interpreting financial statements of Indian companies
Comparing Indian companies — Valuation metrics for sectors like IT, Banking, Pharma, FMCG

modules.title: Fundamental Analysis & Company Research
topics: Introduction to fundamental analysis — Long-term investing approach based on company fundamentals
Industry analysis — Understanding sectors like IT, Banking, Auto, Pharma, and their growth prospects in India
Competitive advantage (Moat) — Identifying companies with sustainable competitive advantages like ITC, Nestlé India
Management quality assessment — Evaluating leadership, track record, and corporate governance of Indian companies
Revenue and profit growth analysis — Tracking 5-year growth trends of Indian company earnings
Debt and leverage analysis — Understanding debt-to-equity ratio and financial stability of companies
Cash flow analysis — Why operating cash flow matters more than accounting profits
Industry cycles and business trends — Recognizing growth phases in Indian sectors and economy
Valuation methods ��� Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), Relative Valuation, and Asset-based valuation for Indian stocks
Reading annual reports and investor presentations — Extracting key information from company documents
Comparing peers — Benchmarking Indian companies against competitors in same sector
Red flags and warning signs — Identifying risky companies and avoiding investment mistakes

modules.title: Technical Analysis & Market Trends
topics: Introduction to technical analysis — Understanding price patterns, trends, and volume for short-term trading
Candlestick charts and patterns — Reading Japanese candlesticks, doji, hammer, engulfing patterns in Indian stocks
Support and resistance levels — Identifying price levels where stocks bounce or break in Indian markets
Trend analysis — Understanding uptrends, downtrends, and sideways movements in stock prices
Moving averages — Using 50-day, 100-day, 200-day moving averages to identify trends
Relative Strength Index (RSI) — Measuring momentum and overbought/oversold conditions
MACD (Moving Average Convergence Divergence) — Using MACD for trend confirmation and reversal signals
Volume analysis — Understanding trading volume and what it tells about price movements
Bollinger Bands — Using volatility bands to identify trading opportunities in Indian stocks
Fibonacci retracement — Using Fibonacci levels to predict support and resistance in trending markets
Chart patterns — Head and shoulders, triangles, flags, and double tops/bottoms in Indian stock charts
Combining indicators — Using multiple indicators together for better accuracy in trading decisions

modules.title: Investment Strategies & Risk Management
topics: Investment vs Trading — Understanding the difference between long-term investing and short-term trading
Value investing strategy — Buying undervalued stocks and holding for long-term wealth creation
Growth investing strategy — Investing in companies with high growth potential in Indian market
Dividend investing strategy — Building passive income through dividend-paying stocks in India
Portfolio diversification — Spreading investments across sectors, market caps, and asset classes
Asset allocation — Dividing portfolio between stocks, bonds, gold, and cash based on age and risk tolerance
Dollar-cost averaging (DCA) — Investing fixed amount regularly to reduce timing risk
SIP (Systematic Investment Plan) — Disciplined investing approach popular in India for mutual funds and stocks
Risk assessment and tolerance — Understanding your risk capacity based on income, age, and financial goals
Stop-loss and profit-taking — Setting exit strategies to protect capital and lock in profits
Position sizing — Determining how much money to invest in each stock based on risk
Emergency fund and insurance — Building financial safety net before starting stock market investing

modules.title: Taxes, Trading Platforms & Practical Investment Execution
topics: Securities Transaction Tax (STT) — Understanding tax on stock purchases and sales in India
Capital gains tax — Short-term capital gains (15% STT) vs long-term capital gains (10% without indexation)
Dividend tax and tax-saving investments — ELSs, NSC, and tax-efficient investing in India
Tax filing for stock market income — Filing ITR forms and reporting capital gains to income tax department
TDS on dividends and interest — Understanding tax deducted at source on investment income
Using trading platforms — Zerodha, Angel Broking, HDFC Securities, and other major platforms in India
Placing buy and sell orders — Market orders, limit orders, and different order types explained
Using screeners and research tools — Finding stocks using BSE/NSE screeners and investment research websites
Reading live market data — Understanding bid-ask spread, volume, open interest, and market depth
Practical case studies — Real examples of successful and failed investments in Indian stocks
Building your first portfolio — Step-by-step guide to selecting and buying stocks worth ₹10,000-50,000
Monitoring and rebalancing portfolio — Regular review and adjusting portfolio allocation quarterly

assessment.theory_format: 25 Questions x 2 Marks (MCQ and Short Answer)
assessment.practical_format: Candidates must complete real-world investment analysis and portfolio creation tasks

practical_items.title: Stock Analysis Report
practical_items.description: Analyze 2 Indian companies (e.g., TCS, HDFC Bank) using fundamental analysis. Submit 5-page report with financial ratios, valuation, competitive analysis, and investment recommendation.

practical_items.title: Technical Analysis Chart Study
practical_items.description: Identify 3 stock charts with support/resistance levels, trend lines, and technical indicators. Provide trading signals and explain your analysis with screenshots.

practical_items.title: Portfolio Creation Project
practical_items.description: Create a diversified portfolio of 5-8 stocks worth ₹50,000 (virtual/paper trading). Justify each selection, show allocation percentages, and explain risk management strategy.

practical_items.title: Tax Calculation Exercise
practical_items.description: Calculate capital gains tax and STT on 5 hypothetical stock transactions. Show all calculations and file ITR format for stock market income.

eligibility: Minimum education: Class 8th pass or equivalent
No prior experience in stock market needed
Basic computer literacy required (internet access, email, online platforms)
Valid ID proof (Aadhaar, PAN, Passport, Voter ID) required at registration
Already working as trader/investor? Apply for direct RPL (Recognition of Prior Learning) exam
Open to all Indian citizens and NRIs with valid documentation
Demat account not mandatory during course but required for practical trading
learning_outcomes: Understand how stock exchanges (NSE/BSE) work and navigate the Indian financial system
Analyze company financial statements and identify undervalued investment opportunities
Apply fundamental and technical analysis to make informed stock selection decisions
Build diversified investment portfolios aligned with personal financial goals and risk tolerance
Execute buy/sell orders on trading platforms and manage real-world stock transactions
Calculate investment returns, capital gains tax, and optimize portfolio for tax efficiency

career_paths.title: Retail Stock Investor
career_paths.salary: Variable income based on portfolio; ₹15,000-50,000/month average returns

course_highlights: Live market case studies using real Indian stocks (TCS, Infosys, HDFC Bank, Reliance, ITC)
Hands-on training on Zerodha, Angel Broking, and other major platforms used in India
Paper trading (virtual trading) practice with real market data before risking real money
Tax planning module specific to Indian income tax rules and capital gains
Guest lectures from successful retail investors and financial advisors
Access to stock screeners, research tools, and market analysis resources
Certification recognized by CETCF for employment in financial sector

course_structure_details.class_duration: 2-3 hours per session, 2-3 sessions per week
course_structure_details.practical_sessions: Live trading terminals, paper trading, portfolio simulation
course_structure_details.teaching_aids: Charts, financial statements, trading platforms, market news, case studies
course_structure_details.reference_materials: NSE/BSE official guides, SEBI investor education materials, financial websites

post_course_support: Lifetime access to course materials and updates
Monthly webinars on market trends and investment strategies
Alumni community for sharing investment ideas and experiences
Job placement assistance with brokerages and financial firms
Continuing education on advanced topics (options trading, derivatives, portfolio management)
